content: The linguistic pedagogical definition of reflexion as both 'reflecting external input' and 'intentional critical self-reflection' reveals that current LLM self-reflection mechanisms are only built to mimic the self-correction half of the full concept, missing the foundational requirement of reflexion as accurate reflection of how the model’s outputs are perceived by external observers. We can frame improved LLM self-reflection as aligning the agent’s internal self-model with the reflected image of its capabilities held by outside users and task environments, rather than only refining outputs through internal critique.
